summary: End-to-end backend + frontend testing of Phase 1/2 Admin features (multi-user auth with forced password change, super-admin Users CRUD, app-settings with Resend key masking + hosts, Host Picker modal on Book CTAs, Stay Tuned empty banners). 19/20 pytest cases pass. 1 backend bug found: 6th failed admin-login attempt returns 500 (timezone-aware vs naive datetime comparison) instead of 429 lockout. All frontend flows verified working.

critical.issue: Lockout path raises TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes. After 5 failed attempts the lockout doc is written with a tz-aware datetime, but on subsequent requests Mongo returns it tz-naive, and the comparison `lockout['locked_until'] > now` (where now is tz-aware) crashes — returning 500 to the user instead of the intended 429. Reproduce by calling /api/admin-login with a valid @orgainse.com email and a wrong password 6 times in a row. Fix: when reading `lockout['locked_until']`, attach UTC tzinfo, e.g. `lu = lockout['locked_until']; if lu.tzinfo is None: lu = lu.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc); if lu > now: ...`. Same pattern likely applies wherever `password_changed_at`, `locked_until`, etc. are compared after a Mongo read.

issues: There are TWO elements containing the text 'Book Consultation' on the homepage — a visually-hidden SEO anchor (Book Consultation - Schedule expert consultation) AND the visible orange nav button. Playwright `getByText('Book Consultation').first` resolves to the invisible anchor, which is brittle for any automation/AT user. Recommend adding aria-hidden=true or sr-only on the SEO anchor, or removing the duplicate.

action_items: FIX backend lockout TypeError on /api/admin-login — read locked_until and coerce to tz-aware UTC before comparison (and audit all other datetime fields read from Mongo: password_changed_at, expires_at). Verify by running the test TestAdminLogin::test_lockout_after_5_fails which currently fails with 500 instead of 429.
Optional polish: hide/remove the duplicate hidden 'Book Consultation' SEO anchor on the homepage so accessibility tooling and tests target the visible CTA.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 2192 lines — should be modularised (routers for admin auth, app-settings, users, blog, newsletter). File size > recommended 700 lines.

Datetime handling inconsistency: locked_until is stored as datetime.now(timezone.utc) but Motor returns naive datetimes by default (no tz_aware=True on AsyncIOMotorClient). This same class of bug can hit must_change_password expiry checks. Consider initialising the Mongo client with tz_aware=True, or wrap all datetime reads in a helper.
The 6-attempt 500 is a real production bug — brute-force protection is silently broken (a determined attacker would just see 500s and keep trying; rate limiter never fires).

success_rate.backend: 95% (19/20 pytest cases pass; 1 critical lockout bug)

test_credentials: swarag@orgainse.com (super-admin), info@/support@/rajesh@orgainse.com (all temp password: Orgainse25%Web..). All four reset back to temp at end of run.
seed_data_creation: Two hosts (Swarag founder + Rajesh director) were written into app_settings.hosts via PUT /api/app-settings during testing; booking_url_default=https://cal.example.com/default. This is required for the host-picker-modal frontend test and should remain configured (or be re-seeded by the main agent's Settings UI if removed).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 seed users were reset to temp password Orgainse25%Web.. and must_change_password=true at end of run. Hosts (Swarag, Rajesh) are configured in app_settings — so HostPickerModal WILL appear on any Book Consultation CTA. To test the no-hosts fallback (window.open direct), clear app_settings.hosts to []. Note: BlogIndex was empty at test time (stay-tuned-blog rendered). NewsletterIndex had 1 published issue, so the stay-tuned-newsletter banner did NOT render — to verify it, delete the published issue first.
rca_of_the_issue:
BUG: 500 on 6th admin-login attempt. ROOT CAUSE: in /app/backend/server.py admin-login handler, `lockout['locked_until']` is read from Mongo (where Motor returns naive datetime by default) and compared against `now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)` (aware). Python raises TypeError on aware-vs-naive comparison. STEPS: (1) POST /api/admin-login with valid @orgainse.com email + wrong password 5 times → 401s + lockout doc written; (2) 6th POST → server reads lockout and crashes with 500. FIX: coerce locked_until to UTC-aware before compare, OR construct AsyncIOMotorClient(tz_aware=True). RECOMMENDED PATCH (illustrative):
    lu = lockout.get('locked_until')
    if lu is not None and lu.tzinfo is None:
        lu = lu.replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    if lu and lu > now:
        raise HTTPException(429, ...)
Audit other reads: password_changed_at, any expires_at fields.
